race.name: Arctic Circle Race
race.tagline: Three days, 160 kilometers, minus twenty-five — sleep in tents on the Greenland ice sheet and ski through the most extreme wilderness race on the planet.

vitals.registration: Application-based, requires demonstrated winter survival skills
vitals.aid_stations: Checkpoint stations at stage boundaries; participants carry mandatory survival equipment
vitals.cutoff_time: Stage-based cutoffs; participants must reach overnight camps before dark

climate.description: Late March in West Greenland brings extreme cold with temperatures routinely dropping to -25°C or below overnight. Daytime temperatures may hover around -10°C to -15°C. Blizzard conditions are possible. Participants sleep in tents on the snow between stages, exposed to the full force of Arctic weather. Wind chill is the primary hazard.
challenges: Overnight tent camping at -25°C or colder between stages
Blizzard risk with whiteout conditions and zero visibility
Wind chill can push effective temperature below -40°C
Mandatory survival gear adds weight to racing kit
Frostbite risk on all exposed skin during three-day effort
Mental endurance tested by isolation, cold, and multi-day fatigue

course.primary: Three-stage point-to-point race through the Greenland wilderness near Sisimiut. The route crosses frozen fjords, glacial plateaus, and Arctic tundra. Terrain is rugged and variable — windswept ice, deep powder, and machine-tracked sections alternate. Significant elevation gain across the three stages with exposed ridgeline crossings.
course.surface: Mixed — snowmobile-tracked trails, windswept ice, ungroomed wilderness sections
course.grooming: Minimal — snowmobile tracks set before each stage, but conditions vary wildly with wind and weather
features: Three-day stage race with tent camping between stages
Crosses frozen fjords and glacial terrain
Mandatory survival equipment including sleeping bag, stove, and emergency rations
Participants from 20+ countries — extreme adventure community
Course marked with flags; navigation skills helpful in whiteout
One of the most extreme organized ski races in the world
Finish in Sisimiut, Greenland's second-largest town

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum scores in distance, elevation, international draw, course technicality, snow reliability, and scenery — this is the most extreme organized XC ski race on the planet. Minimum scores in grooming and accessibility reflect the raw wilderness nature. Tiny field size but intensely international. The tent-camping format is unique in competitive skiing.

history.summary: The Arctic Circle Race was founded in 1998 in Sisimiut, West Greenland. It was created to showcase Greenland's extraordinary Arctic landscape through competitive skiing. The three-day stage format with tent camping was inspired by Arctic expedition traditions, and the race has earned a reputation as one of the most extreme organized endurance events in the world. Despite its small field of around 200, the race draws participants from over 20 countries — adventure athletes and ultra-endurance specialists who seek the ultimate combination of racing and survival.
notable_facts: Participants sleep in tents at -25°C between stages — no lodges or heated shelters
Mandatory equipment list includes sleeping bag rated to -30°C, stove, and emergency food
The course crosses terrain used by Inuit hunters for thousands of years
Sisimiut is Greenland's second-largest town with about 5,500 residents
Whiteout conditions have forced stage modifications in several years
The race attracts special forces soldiers, polar explorers, and ultra-endurance athletes
Greenland's national ski culture is deeply tied to traditional Inuit winter travel
